title: SwiftUI + Agentic AI Roadmap
subtitle: A focused 30-day crash course for iOS engineers preparing SwiftUI, architecture, DSA, LLMs, RAG, and agentic AI with a practical SwiftMigrate Agent project.

strategy.summary: The fastest smart path is not to abandon iOS immediately. Use your iOS experience as leverage, strengthen SwiftUI and architecture, add pattern-based DSA, and build one AI-assisted migration project that proves applied LLM/RAG/agent knowledge.
points: Primary identity: Senior iOS / SwiftUI Engineer.
Differentiator: LLM/RAG/agentic tooling for UIKit to SwiftUI modernization.
Daily split: 50% iOS architecture, 25% DSA, 25% AI project.
Target outcome: interview readiness plus SwiftMigrate Agent V1.
Apply by Day 30 instead of waiting to feel perfect.

tracks.title: Track A — iOS / SwiftUI / Architecture
tracks.summary: Senior-level iOS revision focused on migration, clean architecture, async/await, SwiftUI state, navigation, API layers, and testing.
points: Convert UIKit thinking into SwiftUI state-driven architecture.
Practice MVVM, Service, Repository, UseCase, and Dependency Injection.

tracks.title: Track B — LLM / RAG / Agents
tracks.summary: Applied AI engineering through one useful project instead of random tutorials.
points: Start with prompts and structured outputs.
Add file reading and local Swift code analysis.
Add RAG using your own architecture notes.

tracks.title: Track C — DSA
tracks.summary: Pattern-based DSA to clear product, startup, and GCC interview filters without wasting months.
points: Always explain brute force, optimized approach, time complexity, and edge cases.
Use AI to debug, not to blindly copy.

project.name: SwiftMigrate Agent V1
project.summary: A local AI-assisted tool that reads a UIKit Swift file and generates a structured migration report for SwiftUI, MVVM, async/await, and architecture improvements.
capabilities: Read a UIViewController.swift file from local path.
Detect UIKit patterns like IBOutlet, IBAction, delegates, navigation, table reloads, and completion handlers.
Detect memory risks such as closure retain cycles.
Generate MigrationReport.md with architecture issues and SwiftUI migration suggestions.
Recommend View, ViewModel, Service, Repository, and route structure.
stretchGoals: Generate draft SwiftUI View and ViewModel files.
Add LangGraph workflow: read → analyze → retrieve → report.
project.resumeLine: Built an AI-assisted UIKit to SwiftUI migration analyzer using Python, LangChain, structured LLM prompting, static Swift pattern detection, and RAG-based architecture guidance.

days.title: Swift Value/Reference + LLM Basics
ai: why cost depends on input and output tokens
days.deliverable: Day-01 notes with Swift value/reference examples and LLM/token summary.

days.title: ARC, Retain Cycles, Weak/Unowned
ai: prompt AI to detect Swift memory leaks
ask for fix plus interview explanation
exercises: create and fix a retain cycle
review Swift closure code with AI
days.deliverable: Memory-risk checklist for Swift closures and delegates.

days.title: Closures, Delegates, Protocols, DI
ai: prompt to detect delegates and closure migration opportunities
exercises: convert delegate callback to closure action

days.title: async/await, Task, MainActor
ai: prompt AI to convert completion-handler API to async/await

days.title: SwiftUI State Management
ai: prompt AI to classify UIKit state into SwiftUI wrappers

days.title: NavigationStack + Routing
ai: prompt AI to map UIKit navigation to SwiftUI navigation

days.title: MVVM + Clean Architecture Basics
ai: prompt AI to detect massive ViewController architecture issues
days.deliverable: ServiceRequest architecture skeleton plus interview answer.

days.title: Unit Testing + Mocking ViewModels
exercises: test one ViewModel with mock service

days.title: API Layer, URLSession, Codable, Errors
ai: structured output JSON for code analysis

days.title: Token Refresh + Retry Architecture
ai: prompt AI to detect auth/token risks
days.deliverable: Token refresh architecture diagram and protocols.

days.title: SwiftUI Performance + Identity
ai: prompt AI to detect SwiftUI performance issues
exercises: review one SwiftUI screen for identity/performance issues

days.title: iOS System Design + Agent Architecture
days.deliverable: SwiftMigrate Agent folder structure and README outline.

days.title: Agent File Reading + Static Checks
exercises: run agent on one sample Swift file
days.deliverable: Agent reads one Swift file locally.

days.title: RAG Basics
ios: migration checklist: UI, state, networking, navigation, testing

days.title: Local Retriever Mini Prototype
exercises: retrieve relevant architecture rule before LLM call

days.title: RAG-Powered Migration Prompt
ai: add RAG context to migration prompt
exercises: run migration prompt with retrieved rules

days.title: Evaluate AI Output
exercises: review one AI-generated migration for mistakes

days.title: Generate Draft SwiftUI Files
exercises: generate files for one sample input

days.title: Behavioral Stories + Agent Validation
days.deliverable: Final README draft and behavioral stories.

days.title: Applications + LinkedIn Positioning
ios: apply to senior iOS and AI-mobile roles

days.title: Final Review + Active Switch Plan
days.deliverable: Active applications plus next-phase improvement plan.

questions.title: Swift / iOS Core
items: Explain struct vs class with memory behavior.
What is ARC and how do retain cycles happen?
Difference between weak and unowned?
How do closures capture self?
What is async/await and why is it better than completion handlers?
What is MainActor?
How would you design an API layer with token refresh?
How do you test a ViewModel?

questions.title: SwiftUI
items: When do you use @State vs @Binding?
@StateObject vs @ObservedObject?
When is @EnvironmentObject appropriate?
How does NavigationStack differ from UINavigationController?
How do you handle loading/error/success states?
How do you integrate SwiftUI into UIKit?

questions.title: Architecture
items: Explain MVVM in SwiftUI.
What belongs in ViewModel vs Service vs Repository?
When would you add a UseCase layer?
How does dependency injection improve testability?
How would you migrate a massive ViewController safely?

questions.title: AI / LLM / RAG / Agents
items: What is an LLM?
What are tokens and why do they matter?
What is structured output?
What is RAG and why not send the whole project to the model?
What are embeddings and chunking?
What is an AI agent?
How would your SwiftMigrate Agent work?
What are the risks of AI-generated code?

resources.summary: This site is intentionally data-driven. The page layout lives in index.html, styling in style.css, rendering logic in script.js, and all course content in data/crash-course.json.
items: Update data/crash-course.json to change roadmap content without touching layout.
Use GitHub Pages to host from main branch and /root.
Keep this repo public if it only contains generic learning content and sample code.
Do not add company code, client screenshots, internal API URLs, access tokens, or private architecture documents.
resources.updateRule: For future changes, edit only data/crash-course.json unless you want to change the design or rendering behavior.
